title: sabulina caroliniana
intro: Sabulina caroliniana, also known as the Carolina sandwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sabulina caroliniana include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Sabulina caroliniana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat skin irritations and internally to promote overall well-being.
